title: ZS
text: Ah. Another morning where, against my better judgement, I check my phone, open Instagram, and encounter an artificially intelligent image. Piss filtered, cartoonish yet imprecise imagery. As I created this project, I wanted to ground my research in an almost indescribable feeling that never fails to  beholden me. Words to describe such a feeling include: rage, discomfort, annoyance, and the main focus of this essay,

title: Z Skigen
text:
I am a statistician, baseball analyst, and data scientist with a passion for turning complex problems into actionable insight. My research applies Bayesian causal inference to questions of transportability — asking when and how findings from one population can inform another. My baseball work spans player evaluation, ABS challenge modeling, and building data infrastructure for front offices and college programs alike.
Learn more about me →
